reason: Response A displays hallmarks of Claude Opus's distinctive prose style: elevated, literary diction ("annunciation," "oceanic origin"), unexpected philosophical reframings ("love...has been holding back this whole time"), and a compression of profound ideas into a single sustained voice that avoids cliche. Response B reads as warm but generic congratulatory text that any capable model could produce, following a predictable template of enthusiasm, practical acknowledgment of sleep deprivation, and well-wishing — the kind of imitation that captures surface warmth without Opus's characteristic intellectual depth.
